'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == revision.text:
'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiwara clan-qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiwara clan-qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiwara clan-qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kajiwara clan-qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}

{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} revision.text:
'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially oqqosed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan suqqorted Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court aqqointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, oqqosition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially oqqosed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan suqqorted Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court aqqointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, oqqosition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially oqqosed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan suqqorted Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court aqqointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, oqqosition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In 1180, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially o11osed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan su11orted Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1185, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court a11ointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, o11osition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. === Overview === Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kajiwara {{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp to pp]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

revision.text:
'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=draft}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=draft}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文|12=draft}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=draft}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=draft}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=draft}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=draft}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=draft}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|12=draft}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp to pp]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]]
