'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=draft|12=draft}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft|12=draft}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=draft|12=draft}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=draft|12=draft}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp to pp]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=draft|12=draft}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| }} {{draft In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=draft|12=draft}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft|12=draft}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=draft|12=draft}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=draft|12=draft}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|12=draft|12=draft}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=draft|12=draft}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft|12=draft}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=draft|12=draft}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=draft|12=draft}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=draft|12=draft}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pp]] revision.text:
'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp to pp]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|qq=}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba Kagechika|qq=}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Ishibashiyama|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshū Campaign|qq=}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō clan|qq=}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagesue|qq=}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province|qq=}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pp to pp]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoshifumi|qq=}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}

{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|qq=}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}, though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}, the clan flourished. In pp80, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside {{ill|Ōba Kagechika|ja|大庭景親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba Kagechika|qq=}} during the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Ishibashiyama|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, achieving prominence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}} in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the {{ill|Ōshū Campaign|ja|奥州合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshū Campaign|qq=}}. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the {{ill|Hōjō clan|ja|北条氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō clan|qq=}} and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagesue|qq=}} who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province|qq=}}, serving as retainers of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and served notable figures such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}} (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} - {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|ja|平良文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoshifumi|qq=}} lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}

{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]]

'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]

The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:梶原氏]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]
[[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|qq=}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kantō region]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:梶原氏]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

revision.text:
'''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]
[[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|qq=}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kajiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kajiwara clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:梶原氏]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:梶原氏]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]]
