{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|qq=}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]
[[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|qq=}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|qq=}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|qq=}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]]

[[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]]

The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|qq=|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|qq=|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|qq=|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] }} [[sn:Kajiwara clan]] [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}}

The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|qq=|1=Eight Taira Clans of the East|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425414}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|qq=|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|qq=|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|qq=|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|qq=|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|qq=|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}}

[[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}}

[[ja:梶原氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|1=Eight Taira Clans of the East|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425414}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|1=Kajiwara (Kamakura)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]
[[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|1=Dan Tadamasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420299}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|1=Kamakura no Kagemichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650928}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|1=Kajiwara, Kamakura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}}

This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:梶原氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|1=Eight Taira Clans of the East|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425414}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|1=Kajiwara (Kamakura)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]
[[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
{{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}}
{{ill|Dan Tadamasu|ja|団忠正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Tadamasu|1=Dan Tadamasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420299}} |根拠地= Sagami Province |支流= {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Karakawa clan|ja|唐桑氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakawa clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|1=Kamakura no Kagemichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650928}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|1=Kajiwara, Kamakura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:梶原氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11538831]] Wikidata name: Kajiwara clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kajiwara Clan''' (梶原氏) is a {{ill|Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clans|lt=Japanese clan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most notable lineage is a branch of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, which descends from the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}}, itself part of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} lineage, one of the {{ill|Bandō Hachihei|ja|坂東八平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandō Hachihei|1=Eight Taira Clans of the East|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425414}}. == Taira Lineage of the Kajiwara Clan == {{日本の氏族 |家名= Kajiwara Clan |家紋= Maruni-narabiya.jpg |家紋名称= Maruni Narabiya (丸に並び矢) |本姓= {{ill|Kammu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kammu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} - [[:en:Taira no Yoshifumi|Taira no Yoshifumi]] lineage, branch of the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} via the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}} |家祖= {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja|梶原景久 (平安時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa (Heian period)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |種別= {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地= {{ill|Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja|相模国鎌倉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura District, Sagami Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Kajiwara (Kamakura)|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara (Kamakura)|1=Kajiwara (Kamakura)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}} |人物= [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]]

{{ill|Dan clan|ja|団氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai) and others }} The clan originated with {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Kamakura Kagemichi|ja|鎌倉景通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemichi|1=Kamakura no Kagemichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650928}} of the {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435393}}, who took the name "Kajiwara" from their territory in {{ill|Kajiwara, Kamakura|ja|梶原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara, Kamakura|1=Kajiwara, Kamakura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108779038}}, Sagami Province. The clan rose to prominence during the time of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], though their fortunes later declined. == Overview == Under the leadership of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], the clan flourished. In pp80, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] raised his army, Kagetoki initially opposed him alongside [[:en:Ōba Kagechika|Ōba Kagechika]] during the [[:en:Battle of Ishibashiyama|Battle of Ishibashiyama]]. However, Kagetoki allowed Yoritomo to escape, earning the latter's favor. The Kajiwara clan supported Yoritomo in the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, achieving prominence during the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in pp85, which marked the downfall of the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}. However, Kagetoki's acceptance of court appointments with [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] angered Yoritomo. Despite this, the Kajiwara continued to serve loyally during the [[:en:Battle of Ōshū|Ōshū Campaign]]. After Yoritomo's death, opposition from the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]] and others led to Kagetoki's downfall in the {{ill|Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Incident of Kajiwara Kagetoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538828}}. His descendants, however, continued to serve in various capacities across Japan. === Prominent Branches === * '''Karakawa Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Centered in {{ill|Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja|宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuwa, Kesennuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This settlement includes over 100 households bearing the Kajiwara name, making it the largest concentration of the surname in Japan. The branch descends from {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, Kagetoki's brother, who established {{ill|Kajiwara Shrine|ja|梶原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}} in the region. * '''Shimotsuke Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]] who sought refuge with the {{ill|Utsunomiya clan|ja|宇都宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya clan|1=Utsunomiya clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449780}}. * '''Sanuki Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's third son, Kagemochi, who became sea pirates in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] before serving as retainers to the {{ill|Kuroda Domain|ja|黒田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福岡藩|1=Fukuoka Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208766}}. * '''Musashi Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Another branch of Kagemochi's descendants settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], serving as retainers of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. * '''Owari Kajiwara Clan''' ''' Descendants of Kagetoki's second and third sons, Kageyuki and Kagemochi, who settled in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and served notable figures such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]. === Historical Heads === 1. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagehisa|ja|梶原景久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagehisa|1=Kajiwara Kagehisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538826}} (Founder) 2. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja|梶原景長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagenaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Second head) 3. {{ill|Kajiwara Kagekiyo|ja|梶原景清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagekiyo|1=Kajiwara Kagekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106626560}} (Third head) 4. [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] (Fourth head) 5. {{ill|Kajiwara Tomokage|ja|梶原朝景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Tomokage|1=Kajiwara Tomokage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119345175}} (Fifth head) === Related Clans === * {{ill|Sakawa clan|ja|酒匂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakawa clan|1=Sakawa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644298}} * {{ill|Yakuno clan|ja|役野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ogino clan|ja|荻野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamisaka clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisaka clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650931}} * {{ill|Utsugi clan|ja|宇津木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsugi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/html/about.html About Hayama Shrine] (Official site) {{translated page|ja|梶原氏|version=103398508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:梶原氏]] [[Category:Kajiwara Clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q11538831}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
